name: Maker Faire Downtown Columbia
description: Maker Faire is a gathering of fascinating, curious people who enjoy learning and who love sharing what they can do. From engineers to artists to scientists to crafters, Maker Faire is a venue for these "makers" to show hobbies, experiments, projects. We call it the Greatest Show (& Tell) on Earth - a family-friendly showcase of invention, creativity, and resourcefulness. Glimpse the future and get inspired!

name: Webinar - Web2 to Web3
description: The Crossroad Applications of Blockchain 101 (intersections of Blockchain, Crypto, and AI)

name: Karen Hao — Empire of AI
description: Dreams and Nightmares in Sam Altman's OpenAI - with Bina Venkataraman — at Conn Ave. When AI expert and investigative journalist Karen Hao first began covering OpenAI in 2019, she thought they were the good guys. Founded as a nonprofit with safety enshrined as its core mission, the organization was meant, its leader Sam Altman told us, to act as a check against more purely mercantile, and potentially dangerous, forces. What could go wrong?

name: The Art of Advocacy
description: Stand Up was established on May 11, 2019 by twenty students who were inspired to continue to fight against gun violence after raising awareness about its negative effects for a school project. We even decided to name Stand Up after the poem created for the presentations. Since we established Stand Up, we have accomplished a lot, and we hope to progress further in our change-making journey with your help!

name: From Code to Cure - A Biotech Networking Event
description: Start your morning with innovation, insight, & meaningful connections. Join us for Montgomery College’s signature biotech networking event.

name: Orange Table Talk
description: Stand Up was established on May 11, 2019 by twenty students who were inspired to continue to fight against gun violence after raising awareness about its negative effects for a school project. We even decided to name Stand Up after the poem created for the presentations. Since we established Stand Up, we have accomplished a lot, and we hope to progress further in our change-making journey with your help!

name: Conscious Venture Lab Accelerator Program Cohort 13 Demo Day
description: Meet the eleven startups reimagining the future of capitalism in cohort 13 of the CVL Accelerator Program! We’ve been working with 11 purpose driven founders since March in industries including healthtech, edtech, and fintech on reshaping the future of capitalism and using the power of business to create a more just, joyous, equitable and prosperous society for us all. They are excited to present their innovative technology creating social impact at our program’s culminating event in June. Cohort 13 Demo Day is certain to be an engaging and impactful evening of learning, networking, and showcasing the power of entrepreneurship in Baltimore.

name: PLAITS 2025
description: Privacy & Liberty in the Age of AI & Immersive Technologies.

name: The Economy - Open Discussion [VIRTUAL]
description:
It's the economy, stupid!

This is the place to discuss the **BIG** picture of the modern tech economy. Anything goes! California Capitalism, Military Keynesianism, the United States Dollar, Bitcoin and Etherium, Unemployment, Automation, and what we can do in the Baltimore area to bring prosperity to our people.

Code Collective organizer Julian Coy will be briefly introducing his proposals for the **Baltimore AI Project** and the **Baltimore Dirtbike Project**, programs designed to make use of available facilities to build a strong local economy with less dependence on foreign nations and the federal government.

Do you have special economic understanding or a similar **BIG** project in mind? We would love to hear it in this 2-hour virtual moderated discussion. RSVP for link.

name: Tech Unity Cookout
description:
How pleasant it is when the people of tech come together in unity!

After a year of talking about it, we’re finally doing it: a chill summer cookout for everyone in Baltimore tech. No booths, no buzzwords — just food, sun, and a mic for any group that wants to say hi.

We are hoping to cultivate a virtuous environment of Baltimore's best tech leaders, to share organizational tips, and to lean into Smalltimore's unique social strengths in building the future economy. Ultimately, we hope to foster the relationships that create better lives for ourselves and our people.

Participating groups

* **Code Collective**
* **Baltimore Node Makerspace**
* **Baltimore Tech**
* **Brady UX**
* **DevOps Columbia**
* **Blk Tech Connect**
* **Baltimore Indie Game Devs**

Free admission. Food will be provided!

The event will occur at Wonderground Park in collaboration with the Greenmount West Community Association. It's nearby Guilford Hall Brewery, home of EquiTech Tuesday and occasional host of Baltimore Rhythm Festival.

In the case of inclement weather, the event will occur at Mount Vernon Marketplace, or at a local bar.

name: Baltimore Code & Coffee Demo Day
description:
Our Demo Day is a chance for members of the local tech community to show off the cool projects they’ve been working on. Come and enjoy our participant’s ten minute demos with five minutes for questions.

We'll have demos on 3D Structural Optimization, GTM partnership automation and more.

After the demos, each of the participants will have their own table for you to ask more questions about their work in depth. After 30 mins of this, we will have a vote for the winner.

**SCHEDULE:**
Networking - 11:00am-11:30am
Demos - 11:30am-12:30pm
Table Talk - 12:30pm-1:00pm
Vote 1:00pm-1:10pm
Networking - 1:10pm - 2:00pm

**Venue:**
Digital Harbor Foundation
1045 Light St. Baltimore, MD 21230

**PARKING:** There is metered street parking. If all else fails, park at the West Street Garage. Residents of Baltimore near Charles St should consider the free Charm City Circulator bus.

**HOW TO FIND US**
Please use the main door at 1045 Light Street. If the door is closed, please ring the doorbell.

name: [IN-PERSON] Summer Game Dev Picnic
description:
Come join us for the Baltimore Indie Game Devs potluck picnic at BIG member Allen Lambert's house in Marriottsville just west of Baltimore. This picnic is for all local game devs in the Baltimore-Washington area. Allen will be manning the grill, and there will be outdoor games as well as tabletop games to play.

If you'd like to bring something to the potluck, please add it in the Comments section.

Notes:

* This is a NO ALCOHOL event.
* In the event of rain, we may postpone this event.
* **We'll send the street address to RSVP'd attendees one week before the picnic and the day before the picnic. Please send a message to Allen Lambert or Tony Li if you'd like the address sooner.**

name: All Levels: Online Ask Me Anything WordPress!
description:
Ask Us Anything WordPress!

Register for it by filling out this form: https://forms.gle/Th6AZUbS2GoiCEJz5

This is a virtual meetup on Zoom for you to ask your WordPress questions and help you fix problems with your website.

The answerer for this meetup will be Gen Herres, owner of Anphira Web Design & Development, and a WordPress veteran of over a decade. She's built and remodeled hundreds of WordPress sites.

Don't have a question? You can still come and see what others ask - you might learn something!

We're asking you to submit questions in advance (please use the form at https://forms.gle/Th6AZUbS2GoiCEJz5) so that we can gather common ones together to answer them. But if you'd like to share your screen when it's your turn during the meetup, just let us know on the registration form!

You'll be muted on entry, we'll be using the chat to queue people up/ask follow-on questions.

The meetup will run 11am ET until 12pm ET or until we finish up the questions, whichever comes first. If you pre-submitted your question and are not present, then your question will be skipped.

Meetup is scheduled to be recorded. Recordings are posted about 2 weeks after the meetup and are provided courtesy of our sponsor, [Obee Digital Consulting](https://obeedigitalconsulting.com/).

How to get notified about the recording: either subscribe to the [youtube channel](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLoT0wv53Bj-BGfD8fPhMRC3qTtPMZHioI), RSVP "yes" to the meetup, or [join the mailing list](https://share.mailercloud.com/form?id=a0NzdUAxMTI1OUAwMDAwMA==).

name: Techstars AI Health Baltimore Demo Day
description:
Calling all Baltimore startup champions and Techstars allies! Mark your calendars for June 5th, 2025 and join us at M&T Bank Stadium to celebrate the debut of the inaugural class of the flagship accelerator - Techstars AI Health Baltimore powered by Johns Hopkins University and CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield.  

Be there as our cohort of ambitious founders demonstrates their innovative approaches to improving healthcare solutions. Get ready for a fast-paced evening with guest speakers, live pitches, networking, and complimentary food and drink.

If you’d like, take a peek at the Techstars AI Health Baltimore Spring 2025 cohort in advance:

• AidRx (Calgary, AB, Canada)

• Behaivior (Pittsburgh, PA, USA)

• Compose Health (Ottawa, ON, Canada)

• Embryoxite (Buenos Aires, Argentina)

• Galen Health (Windsor, OH, USA)

• Opal (Tampa, FL, USA)

• Sloop (New York, NY, USA)

• Valio (Indianapolis, IN, USA)

__________________________________________________

​​​A tremendous thank you to our partners - Johns Hopkins University and CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield. 

Our founders benefit greatly from Johns Hopkins' expertise in translating research into viable businesses and CareFirst's deep understanding of delivering accessible, equitable, and high-quality healthcare, not to mention our partners' unparalleled commitment to Baltimore City!

We also appreciate support from Brex, a corporate Techstars partner.

By clicking registering for this event, you are agreeing to our Terms of Use, Privacy Policy, and Media Release. If you have questions please reach out to privacy@techstars.com.​​​Techstars Privacy PolicyTechstars Terms of UseTechstars Media Release

name: Baltimore Climate Tech Meetup June 2025
description:
Join the Baltimore Climate Tech community for our June networking meetup at Union Brewery. Whether you're a climate veteran or looking to transition, join us to meet new friends and colleagues to chat about sustainability and climate tech.

​These happy hours often include a few dozen of us working in Climate Tech, including founders, funders, builders, and people looking to learn more about transitioning into Climate Tech in and around the Baltimore area.

​Let's learn from, support, and inspire each other around technology aimed at combating our climate crisis.

​Please share our events with friends and colleagues as we grow the Baltimore Climate Tech community with your help!

calendar.name: Baltimore Climate Tech Meetups
calendar.description: We host monthly gatherings for anyone interested, working, investing, and buliding in climate tech around Baltimore.

featured_guests.name: Kristen Shaw
featured_guests.bio:
Kristen Shaw is a seasoned growth marketing strategist with two decades of experience driving results for tech companies and startups. She combines strategic vision, data-driven analysis, and adaptability to deliver impactful growth. 

Kristen's expertise spans demand generation, content marketing, and building high-performing teams, while optimizing processes for efficiency and scalability.

Currently serving as the National Marketing Chair for Cleantech Open and Head of Growth for Eristem. 
Kristen is focusing her skills on climate tech and sustainability initiatives. She brings a unique perspective to businesses looking to make an impact while driving growth, calmly navigating the rapid changes and ambiguity inherent in the evolving climate sectors.

registration_questions.question: How has the Baltimore Climate Tech Meetup benefited you? Catalytic connections, peer support, new knowledge, or more? Please share a story of the value our group has given you.
Please share your phone number and we will add you to our WhatsApp group.
